name: Activity Gap Detector - Backstory Starter
description: Compares each rep's weekly activity patterns against team benchmarks and top performer profiles using Backstory activity data. Identifies reps with low outbound activity, thin multi-threading on key deals, or single-threaded opportunities missing executive engagement. An AI agent generates personalized coaching nudges for sales managers, highlighting specific gaps and suggesting actionable improvement areas. Delivered weekly to frontline managers via Messaging.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch per-rep activity totals for the last week and include manager ownership, open pipeline, and multi-threading metadata. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch per-rep activity totals for the last week and include manager ownership, open pipeline, and multi-threading metadata.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw rep activity rollups responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze Activity Gap Detector

parameters.text:
=You are generating the Activity Gap Detector workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Compares each rep's weekly activity patterns against team benchmarks and top performer profiles using Backstory activity data. Identifies reps with low outbound activity, thin multi-threading on key deals, or single-threaded opportunities missing executive engagement. An AI agent generates personalized coaching nudges for sales managers, highlighting specific gaps and suggesting actionable improvement areas. Delivered weekly to frontline managers via Messaging.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the activity gap detector use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
📉 **Weekly Activity Gap Report** — @manager.jen's Team | Week of Mar 3

📊 **TEAM BENCHMARKS:**
- Avg emails/week: 47 | Top performer: 72 (@sarah.chen)
- Avg meetings/week: 8 | Top performer: 12 (@james.park)
- Avg accounts touched: 11 | Top performer: 16 (@sarah.chen)

🔴 **SIGNIFICANT GAPS:**
- **@david.kim** — 3 gaps identified
- Emails: 18/week (62% below team avg of 47)

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating Activity Gap Detector outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
